title: Level 880
wikitext:
{{Fix|date=4/21/2026|this page suffers from the following issues: SP-001 image is AI generated.}} {{VoidedTheme}} {{Background|file=Black screen.png}}
'''Content Warning'''
This page contains sensitive content that may not be suitable for all audiences. Readers including you might consider these topic(s) disturbing, anxiety inducing, or inappropriate: '''Mentions of suicide.'''
[[File:new spectrophobia image.jpg|1000x523px]]
---- {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 880}}
S is for
Spectrophobia
[[File:fancy_line_header.png|690px]]
{{Class Undetermined}} '''Level 880''' is the 881st level in the Backrooms. ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=new spectrophobia image.jpg|caption=An image of the level, dating back to 2008. Photographer missing.}} {{Blurimage|file=Forgot.jpg|caption=A staircase in ''''Level 880''''. The photographer is confirmed dead.}} '''Level 880''', colloquially referred to as "Spectrophobia", manifests as an enigmatic nexus of corridors and rooms bearing a resemblance to a suburban residence, comparable to several other levels within the Backrooms. The entire level appears noticeably old, with outdated furniture and a profusion of dust and wear on many surfaces. Shrouding the area is a light gray fog, which, combined with the absence of functional lights throughout the level, renders navigation an arduous task if one does not carry their own light source. The main design that comprises the level complements this darkness, with walls painted in shades of gray, desaturated blue, and yellow. The flooring is also constructed of either wood or light gray carpeting with various patterns. Reflecting the aged appearance of the items and composition within the entirety of '''Level 880''', almost every surface in the level seems to be weak in structure, rotting, or shattered altogether. One's visual surroundings in '''Level 880''' are described as having low contrast and brightness, with multiple reports noting that the environment appears as if these colorimetric properties had been reduced in a photo editor. Additionally, the saturation of the level seems to decrease depending on the sanity of the wanderer. However, this anomaly has been confirmed to be solely perceptual, meaning the actual attributes of the level's surroundings remain unchanging. Noise is suppressed to a significant extent at this level, with recorded noise levels ranging from 10 to 14 decibels. The lack of noise is often compared to the audible noise level one experiences while wearing earmuffs or similar noise-cancelling devices, and multiple individuals report feelings of vertigo, disorientation, and nausea when exposed to it for an extended period. The only inconsistencies in the noise level are occasional anomalous sounds emanating from an unknown source; these noises can vary but typically include footsteps, screams, knocking, doors opening, and laughing, among many others. The sole knowledge of the sounds being able to be heard at an unknown time often induces severe apprehension in wanderers, with many noting this apprehension will drastically speed up the effects of [[Phenomenon 35]]. Due to this, it is heavily recommended to exit the level in the smallest period of time possible to avoid succumbing to these effects. The architecture of '''Level 880''', while maintaining consistency with the environment it imitates, is quite peculiar; for instance, stairs may ascend at an incline too steep to use, walls curve in unnatural ways, and light switches are placed on the floor. Furniture may be abnormally elongated or shortened, and certain areas' access points may be too narrow to pass through. The level appears to duplicate itself for each individual who enters, with each iteration structuring itself differently from the others; mapping the locations of these irregularities to guide other wanderers through the level is therefore impractical. Strangely, however, items that have been left in '''Level 880''' by other wanderers will remain in the level after their demise or egress; as a result of this, it is not uncommon to find resources dispersed around the expanse of this level. Additionally, recordings of the level indicate that the area around where the items were left remains unchanged, seemingly unaffected by the structural changes. It is worth noting that, in extremely rare cases, the zones surrounding these abandoned items may overlap with separate instances of the level. If multiple wanderers are inside the level at the same time, they may encounter one another, at which point the overlapping regions will merge, allowing all affected individuals to occupy them simultaneously. However, this phenomenon cannot occur if the level's main effect has already been initialized for any instance. As implied by '''Level 880''''s nickname, the level's primary attribute and peril are the abundance of anomalous mirrors scattered throughout it. Each mirror has a simple shape and structure, typically composed of only a few basic shapes rather than intricate or vintage patterns or designs. In contrast to the rest of the level, the surfaces of each mirror are immaculate. Additionally, they seem impervious to damage by any means, and any stains applied to the mirror will disappear when contact with it is broken. As mentioned earlier, the aspects that the mirrors hold make their appearance stand out against the rest of the level's environment, reportedly attracting wanderers out of curiosity. Under no circumstances should an individual position themselves in an area where any part of their body is visible within a mirror's reflection. Incompliance will initiate an effect referred to as the "Kathrethanatos Process". ==Kaftrethanatos Process==
'''M.E.G. Notice''' If you have unfortunately triggered the commencement of this process, we kindly request that you record your experience at this level. Your recording device will be picked up by an agent following your demise during one of our scouting missions, which can be used to help strengthen our understanding of "'Level 880'". Your life will end either way, so you might as well make use of the remaining time you have left.
{{Blurimage|file=Deterioration.jpg|caption=An image of the level during the Deterioration phase, likely with enhanced brightness.}} Derived from the Greek words "Kaftrethis" and "Thanatos", translating to "mirror" and "death" respectively, the Kathrethanatos Process refers to a sequence of events that, in every recorded occurrence, has led to a fate widely regarded as one of the worst fates to ever exist in the Backrooms. As previously mentioned, the process begins when an individual becomes visible in the reflection of any mirror on this level, at an angle of approximately 100 degrees in front of it. The entirety of the Kaftrethanatos Process has been divided into five distinct phases, each becoming progressively more hazardous as time passes since the process was initiated. It is essential to note that the process is not fully understood and may not be fully documented; all currently known information has been obtained through cameras, microphones, and text. The individuals conducting these investigations are those convicted of crimes or harmful acts against the [[M.E.G.]] or [[The Kauer Research Organization]]. They are subsequently forced into the role of a "field researcher" in a collaborative operation involving the two aforementioned groups. Although this is a controversial topic among wanderers across the Backrooms, it is a necessary practice for solidifying understanding of '''Level 880'''. The documented parts of the Kaftrethanatos Process are as follows:
===Initiation=== The first phase of the Kaftrethanatos Process is known as the "Initiation" stage, occurring directly after one steps into the visible range of a mirror's reflection. During this phase, exits from this level will immediately cease all functionality, most commonly in the form of the exit doors locking. There is a complete lack of any reliable indication that suggests the process has commenced in this stage. ===Deterioration=== The second phase of the Kaftrethanatos Process has been coined "Deterioration", occurring approximately 30 minutes subsequent to the previous stage. While Deterioration is occurring, parts of the level will begin to break; this includes sections of the ceiling crumbling and breaking off, furniture falling over, and an abundance of thick dust and mold spreading across the entirety of the level's environment. Ambient noises will slowly begin to emanate more consistently, and the level will become significantly more devoid of light, rendering navigation completely infeasible if one does not carry any light sources of their own. Additionally, the level's size increases significantly, with some cases reporting that its size becomes infinite. The falling debris and environmental hazards pose a moderate threat to individuals during this phase. However, they can be mitigated by sheltering in place, much like one would during an earthquake. There have been a minimal number of confirmed deaths during this phase, though it is still advised to attempt to avoid as much impact from the debris as possible. ===Stagnation=== The third phase of the Kaftrethanatos Process is known as "Stagnation", initiating at the 40-minute mark of the process. Following the advent of this phase, all ambient noises and decay will instantaneously cease, with reports describing it as the level seemingly stopping time. The phase solely exists as an "intermission" between the previous and next phase, lacking any notable events entirely. ===Revelation=== The fourth phase of the Kaftrethanatos Process, known as "Revelation," occurs exactly 50 minutes after the initial triggering of the effect. Throughout the duration of this phase, all the mirrors within '''Level 880''' cease the reflection of reality; what is seen in the mirror instead of what one would expect has been reported multiple times as one of the most horrifying images ever visualized. In addition to representing creatures within their reflection bearing unsettling facial features, the level also seems to create immensely disturbing depictions of oneself having been mutilated, tortured, and forced into gruesome states by indeterminate means. The significant visual horror created by the anomalies in the mirror's reflection causes extreme feelings of anxiety, dread, and overall hopelessness in every reported instance. Additionally, cameras strangely fail to register what is shown in the mirror, so it is currently unknown whether the anomalies present in the reflection are hallucinations. ===Calamity=== The fifth phase of the Kaftrethanatos Process is known as "Calamity". Unlike the prior phases of the process, Calamity has no fixed starting time; instead, it occurs immediately after one looks into a mirror during the preceding phase. Following the commencement of the phase, individuals notice a noticeable increase in unnerving ambient sounds, occurring at significantly shorter intervals than during the Deterioration phase. The main defining characteristic of Calamity is an incursion containing a multitude of previously undiscovered entities from an indeterminate location. While a wide variety of them exist, each creature shares physical traits, including extremely dark skin, a somewhat humanoid body, and an absence of distinguishing bodily features. Additionally, each entity has an extremely high level of aggression, with intentions to physically and/or psychologically harm individuals in the level to a significant extent. The underlying reason for this behavior is currently unknown and is being studied and investigated through the means mentioned above. What occurs after Calamity is unknown, as there have been no reported cases of any individual surviving long enough to prove the existence of subsequent phases.
In addition to each anomalous property occurring in each phase, several effects have been identified that are present throughout the entire process. These include: *Feelings of thirst that seemingly will not cease, regardless of one's current hydration. This is mainly seen as an annoyance rather than an actual threat, as it causes no real harm to one's physical state. *Intense feelings of demotivation and resignation, completely eradicating all desire to attempt survival, as well as all hope of exiting the level. Individuals who have successfully escaped '''Level 880''' have reported that these emotions feel as if they stem from the feeling that they serve no significant purpose and are not accepted by anyone. *The inability to sustain severe physical harm by one's own intentional actions. The prevention of this usually manifests as firearms becoming jammed, sudden waves of fatigue washing over oneself prior to doing a dangerous action, or simply not having inflicted damage in the first place. It is unknown why this happens, and it is thought that the amount of information known about this subject will remain in this state indefinitely. To avoid triggering the process, it is advisable for wanderers to fully cover themselves with items such as blankets, tarps, or similar materials that can provide protection. It is worth noting that doing this does not guarantee one's survival, but rather heightens the chance of it at the cost of visibility. Unfortunately, a reliable method to safely exit the level has not been discovered, and the chances are very improbable that one will successfully manage to egress '''Level 880'''. ==Entities== The only entities confirmed to be inhabiting the expanse of '''Level 880''' are presumed to be solely level-exclusive, aside from a small number of unconfirmed reports where wanderers claim to have sighted various occurrences of [[Entity 6|Dullers]]. The documented entities have been designated under the classification format "SP-###", where the first two letters are named after the level's given nickname, and the three numbers proceeding them represent the chronological order in which they were documented. Though researchers estimate that around 50 to 70 species occupy '''Level 880''', only 3 consistently reappearing specimens have been studied and researched. It is believed that each is closely related genetically, much like the relationships within a species and those to others classified under the same genus. While each entity is guaranteed to hunt following the initiation of the Kaftrethanatos Process, there are extremely rare cases where entities will appear if individuals remain in '''Level 880''' for an extended time period. The place where each entity enters an instance during the Kaftrethanatos Process is unconfirmed, as they seem to enter the level whilst a wanderer is not looking randomly. |-| SP-001= ==='''SP-001 - Voidgaze'''=== {{Blurimage|file=Voidgaze.png|caption=The first sighting of SP-001.}} SP-001, colloquially referred to as a "Voidgaze", appears as a creature that takes on a humanoid shape, bearing deep black skin and an abundance of eyes placed in abnormal locations throughout the entirety of its body. Voidgazes are approximately seven and a half feet, or 2.3 meters, in height, rendering each occurrence to be unnaturally taller than the average human being. SP-001 is typically found crouching in the corners of a room, though in some cases it can reside inside of closets, cabinets, or within other small spaces. Additionally, the entity's low noise while moving, along with its body blending into the overall lack of lighting in '''Level 880''', make spotting it difficult. SP-001 is extremely fast, sometimes reaching recorded speeds of up to thirty miles per hour, or approximately forty-eight kilometers per hour, surpassing the speed of the fastest human as of now. The entity was originally sighted on April 19th in the year 2008, although the entity's existence was not entirely confirmed until over five years after the initial sighting. The proof of SP-001's actuality exists in a photographic form, taken by ██████ █████, a controversial figure at the time who leaked classified information surrounding [[M.E.G.]] 's more illicit operations contemporaneously. Research operations intended to gather more information on SP-001 were approved around this time and continued over the following three years, only stopping when the public began to grow suspicious of the significant increase in individuals going missing without a trace. While the [[M.E.G.]] promptly dismissed these claims as conspiracy theories, all research operations ceased to be carried out immediately, with none starting for the next decade. Occurrences of SP-001 remain immobile when first spotted by an individual in the fifth phase of the Kaftrethanatos Process, aside from the erratic ocular movements around their body. Concurrently, wanderers will experience extreme unease, with many reports claiming the feeling's intensity is so great that it physically pains the affected individual. In rare cases, especially for those sensitive to disturbing images, moving can become strenuous. Strangely, however, the severity of these sensations will fade if the wanderer looks away, although this is highly discouraged; the entity will instantly enter a state of extreme hostility when individuals whom it knows are in its presence break eye contact. SP-001's attack method includes seemingly vanishing while not in one's view before proceeding to lunge at its victims' faces and emit a deafening scream, recorded at exactly 231 decibels for every instance, greatly surpassing the maximum decibel limit in Earth's atmosphere; the noise SP-001 creates is so loud that individuals are instantly killed by it. Cameras do not stop the entity from attacking, and using this, researchers confirmed the aforementioned "vanishing" segment of their attack is simply the entity crawling out of the area its victim is in at an inhumanly fast speed, rather than staying still within the camera's field of view. While SP-001's movements are unpredictable, two methods to defend oneself against instances of the entity exist. The first method involves attacking it while it is immobile, as it is extremely susceptible to damage. Notably, this action is likely to attract other entities, regardless of the assailant's audibility. The second option is much safer, requiring only one step outside the SP-001's line of sight. This will confuse the entity and cause it to begin searching for the victim, although it can easily be avoided by hiding. |-| SP-002= ==='''SP-002 - Leporidus'''=== {{Blurimage|file=bnuuy eeee.jpg|caption=An image of SP-002, taken a few moments before the photographer's death. }} Designated the name of "Leporidus" due to its appearance being somewhat resemblant of a rabbit, SP-002 is the second discovered entity in '''Level 880''', with its physical composition taking the appearance of a slightly warped humanoid bearing two long, pointed horn-like shapes fixed atop its head. SP-002's body does not bear any facial features or appendages apart from its horns; additionally, both of SP-002's arms end in unnaturally sharp points. The height of each instance of SP-002 is not consistent, though each documented occurrence is within the range of 5 to 7 feet (1.5 to 1.8 meters) tall, with its horns taking up exactly 1 foot for each one. SP-002, while in an unprovoked state, slowly patrols the level's corridors at a speed of what can be considered an average walking pace. SP-002, in addition, emits little to no sound, and the only method discovered for recognizing its presence is by the small traces of black hair left behind in the areas it travels. The entity will sometimes be found lying on the floor, as if it were deceased. It is strongly advised to stay as far away from occurrences of SP-002 enacting this action, as it is nothing more than a trap to kill its victims. The first confirmed sighting of an instance of SP-002 was in the year 2019, taking place while the suspension of all '''Level 880''' expeditions was still in effect. The discovery of SP-002 is documented in videographic evidence, taken by an individual whose identity is unknown. The tape was not recovered until the suspension ended, when it was discovered during an expedition conducted by [[The Kauer Research Organization|K.R.O.]] agents in late 2021; the video was found by Agent █████ ██████████ under a broken table near the level's exit. The contents of this tape are confidential, as almost every individual who has viewed the videotape committed suicide shortly after. Each person affected displayed signs of extreme dread and anxiety, presumably as a result of an anomalous effect from SP-002. A reliable method to view the tape without succumbing to this anomaly has not been discovered and is currently being studied. Until then, viewing of the tape is strictly prohibited, and it will not be released to the public eye until further notice. SP-002, following its sighting of prey, expeditiously enters a state of pursuit, chasing them at speeds of up to twelve miles per hour, depending on the number of obstacles in its path. Correspondingly to its unprovoked state, SP-002 will produce almost zero sound, with its audibility comparable to the rustling of leaves. The pursuit only stops once its victim is out of its line of sight for more than a few seconds, though allowing this to happen is extremely challenging due to its high stamina. In the event that an SP-002 occurrence successfully manages to reach its victims, it will quickly puncture their skin using the points on their arms, subsequently tearing off parts of their flesh; finally, the assault is ceased shortly after to allow the victim to die from blood loss. It is an excruciating way to perish, and sadly, no recorded cases of any of SP-002's targets escaping once they have been captured have been documented. Excluding the aforesaid evasion method that involves remaining outside of its field of view for a brief period of time, there exist no other techniques one can utilize to protect themselves from SP-002. The entity seems to be invulnerable to any form of detriment, and it is not recommended for one to attempt to attack SP-002, as it is very probable that it will reach them before the chance of this is plausible. |-| SP-003= ==='''SP-003 - Nullrunner'''=== {{Blurimage|file=SP-003.png|caption=A photograph of SP-003.}} SP-003, informally referred to using the term "Nullrunner", takes on the appearance of a pale, distorted humanoid face that can be compared to a young female. Unlike the previous entities under the SP classification, only one instance of SP-003 exists, rather than multiple inhabiting the level. SP-003 appears to be engulfed in a thick, black fog that mysteriously leaves only its face visible; however, every person who has seen the entity claims to have seen its body, although its configuration cannot be described coherently by any witness. The reason recording equipment is unable to capture the entity's body is currently unknown, although it is thought that the quality of each recording is too low to do so. Additionally, the body cannot be properly viewed through a camera with a flashlight, as the fog somehow reflects light. SP-003, on average, makes an attempt to attack the wanderer in the instance it inhabits roughly four times every two hours. It appears to travel throughout the level at a consistent speed of approximately thirty-six miles per hour, its face remaining unnaturally still. The entity has been observed to be entirely blind; instead of using optical means, it appears to navigate and map out the environment of '''Level 880''' by relying on the fog surrounding its body. In addition, SP-003 has never been viewed as being in a state of inaction. SP-003's presence can be determined by the sounds it emits. The sound has been described as a distorted scream that grows progressively louder as the entity approaches. With the combined factors, SP-003 has been widely accepted as the easiest entity to avoid, solely requiring that one hide from it or, if possible, stay out of proximity to its fog. While SP-003 is the entity that has been most recently confirmed, its existence was first recorded following the lift of the expedition ban, specifically on December 28th, 2021. Its appearance was initially observed using the photo shown above, which was discovered in the same hallway it depicts. Researchers discovered the photo during the same expedition that led to the discovery of SP-002. The entity was not documented until 2024, when an individual named Morton Ford unintentionally noclipped into '''Level 880'''. While attempting the use of [[Phenomenon 2|BACK-NET]] to determine his current location, SP-003 emerged from around a corner and started moving towards him. Morton avoided death from SP-003 and subsequently wrote a note explaining his understanding of the entity, accompanied by a sketch. Morton's current whereabouts are unknown, as there have been no further signs of his existence to date. It is widely accepted that Morton perished in '''Level 880''', and it has been considered pointless to attempt searching for him. ==Colonies & Outposts== Due to the nature within '''Level 880''', colonies and outposts are unable to be established. ==Addendum==
Recovered Note:
''Deceit''

Breathing heavily, I slowly moved my hand towards the wall. The rough, pale texture reminded me of other levels where I could spend my days, living a carefree existence, oblivious to the horrors that make up this universe we call The Backrooms. I used to call [[Level 231]] my home. Pretty funny considering its nickname. Every day, I'd wake up, gather all my supplies, and explore the nexus of halls devoid of anyone besides myself. I would live off items I found in random places. It was a wearisome existence, but it was better than nothing. I would get to explore an infinite house I had all to myself and enjoy the odd environments I would come across. It was an empty life - I had not seen a person for what seemed like years, though I was enjoying myself as much as I possibly could. I wish I could still feel this sense of safety and comfort. I remember opening my eyes one day. The lights above me hummed softly, and the carpet below me felt soft and welcoming. I lifted my head off the ground and took a quick look around. There were two long, narrow staircases on opposite sides of the room and a door before me. The items I had scavenged while in this level were neatly organized next to my backpack, precisely as I had left them before I fell asleep. I moved my arm out towards my pile of [[Object 1|Almond Water]] and grabbed a can off the top. The room was quiet and serene, the absence of noise halted only by my breath. I quickly sat up, opened the can, and drank the refreshing golden liquid. My mind felt replenished, and the tension in my body disappeared limb by limb. I then packed up my belongings and stuffed them into my backpack however I could. I put on the bag and slowly opened the door. I don't know what I expected. Behind the door was yet another series of corridors I had yet to explore. There were a few pieces of furniture, although they seemed dusty and worn. My eyes glanced around the room, observing my surroundings, before noticing a painting on the wall. The brushstrokes came together to depict a vibrant landscape with mountains, in contrast to the uninviting environment of [[Level 231]]. Curious, I grabbed the painting and tried to remove it from the wall. It wouldn't budge, and the weak grip I had on it, thinking it would be easy to remove, caused me to stumble backward. And then I blacked out. When I opened my eyes again, my body felt as if I had been moved without ever waking up. My body lay still on a cold, smooth surface. The radiance of the surrounding environment flooded my eyes with blinding light as a soft breeze glided through the expanse I resided within. Gentle rainfall purified the air, each breath clouding my mind with feelings of tranquility, as if painting a placid landscape to soothe my mind - each inhalation acting as a slow, deliberate brushstroke. It would only make sense if I had been in a state of senselessness for hours on end, as the time before my eyes were able to orient themselves to the drastic change in environment was incalculable. Only then was I able to truly appreciate the beauty of everything around me. Light, gray clouds slowly drifted across the sky above, and the celestial body mimicking the sun illuminated each one. Finally standing up, I slowly took in every part of the world around me. An immense, mountainous scene encompassed me, foliage covering the landscape and filling it with life; a serene world so perfectly crafted as if with the touch of an angel. I stood atop a footbridge, a vitric composition hanging from the side of a mountain, its aridity contrasting with the lush flora nestling around it. The bridge was suspended miles above the ground below. Shaking my head, I snapped back to reality; I had no clue where I was. There wasn't even a remote possibility that any technology would help me up here, let alone my complete absence of any cognizance relating to my whereabouts. And so, with nothing else to do, the only option was to walk along the glass paths until I found an egress from this place. If this were a movie, I would have just entered one of those scenes where the camera slowly zooms out, continuing until it transitions to the next part of the plot that is relevant; after all, it would be pointless to film someone journeying through a colossal environment for hours on end. One can never truly understand how grateful they are that cinematography is this way until they experience one of these moments in person. There was no helicopter to airlift me to the following prominent scene in my life — only my body, walking for miles and miles on an endless, otherworldly terrain. I could once again describe the sense of equanimity I felt in the surroundings in significant depth, but all of these feelings were replaced by doubt and insecurity. Everything felt too perfect – almost as if the world around me was trying too hard to invoke an atmosphere. Every step served as a reminder of just how hopeless I really was. There were no people, no animals, and no possibility of either. Sometimes I stop and think to myself, wondering if anyone has even a fragment of me within their memory. I had left reality so long ago that I don't even remember the faces of my loved ones. Everyone I knew most likely has accepted my death. I don't even know if they're still alive. I try not to think about it because it makes me feel immense guilt when I do. I know it's not my fault, but it feels that way. There have been so many times that I felt like I would fall. I could accidentally stumble off the edge of the bridge or obliterate one of the glass panels. All I want to do is get down instead of being limited in movement by these narrow platforms. Maybe I would survive if I jumped. I doubt this path is finite, but there's no way to tell. There's a chance that the edge is right around the corner, and an equal chance that I am simply a grain of sand trying to escape a black hole. I took a breath, but the atmosphere no longer felt calming. The environment around me was toying with my mental stability like I was its doll. I must have been walking for over three days, and my legs felt like they were about to give out. Everything had been in a perpetual state of daylight from the moment I entered, but suddenly, as I saw it for the first time, it rejuvenated my body, and a wave of relief washed over me. The bridge ceased to extend into the distance, and I sprinted towards the horizon like I was escaping from prison. And just as quickly as the relief started, it ended. The sight that met my gaze was an old, dilapidated staircase extending down farther than the eye could see. I thought I had reached the exit after so long, and yet I was back in the same situation I'd entered this place in. Infinity doesn't have an end, after all. The end of these glass paths was an equally grueling staircase, but it was an exit nonetheless. I spent hours deciding what to do. I didn't want to turn back. I didn't want to jump. I didn't want to remain in this spot until I expired. So, with nothing left to do, I began my slow, long descent into the abyss below. The last thing I expected was to meet other individuals after being tortured by the unending isolation of [[The Backrooms]] for so long. I can't describe the shock that washed over me when two of them ascending the staircase in front of me came into view. Without hesitation, I sprinted towards them as fast as I could. The two looked up at me, with one motioning for me to approach. I could hear one of them quietly say, "Jesus Christ, finally," in a monotonous tone of voice, reaching into his pocket and pulling out a piece of paper. His eyes slowly made their way down the document. "The Major Explorer Group is currently seeking volunteers to participate in a research and exploration project. While the program consists of many components, your role will be to conduct a solo field mission to collect loose items. "As of now, we are experiencing operational delays due to a shortage of personnel assigned to this mission. Your involvement would directly support this project and greatly aid us in gaining a deeper understanding of the properties of the aforementioned location. Participants who successfully complete this task will be formally recognized and rewarded. Compensation includes a designated space suitable for habilitation and a stable, ongoing supply of essential resources, distributed and maintained by the Major Explorer Group. Once involved with the program, compliance is mandatory. Participation is voluntary, but due to the current shortage of volunteers, this may need to change in the near future if the shortage is not resolved soon. We greatly appreciate your consideration." Drawn to the offer by the prospect of claiming the reward, I accepted it without hesitation. I had never felt more motivated or important. The level I was assigned to greeted me with an unlit household corridor, my body upright atop a murky, gray carpet textured with a grid pattern in a lighter shade. The walls and ceiling were painted light gray, their brightness masked by the darkness that engulfed the level. The abundance of mirrors lining the walls caught my eye, each one bearing no residue or wear, as if someone had prepared them for my arrival. The walkie-talkie I carried with me buzzed to life, a voice emanating from its speaker. "Alright, I'm going to need you to have a look at one of the mirrors for me, as what appears on it will allow me to tell you what to do next." Those words broke me. Multiple people have told me a research team combining two groups had forced people they knew into an inescapable death involving mirrors, more formally, the location known as '''Level 880''', as a punishment for crimes against them. But it wasn't just the people who were deserving of this fate that were condemned to this. There was no opportunity of a lifetime for me. I wouldn't be rewarded. I thought I would feel accomplished. Instead, they put me in this hellhole where every single action I make has to be done to a level of perfection lest my life go to waste. They took advantage of my hope and shattered it into pieces. I threw the walkie-talkie on the floor and crushed it with my foot out of a sense of betrayal. I had always been sensitive about breaking perfectly good pieces of technology, and my action only made me feel a deeper sense of hopelessness. I thought I was important. I was merely a test dummy. Deep down, I know they want me to die. They want me to activate whatever process is woven into the walls. I was tasked with finding abandoned items left by souls left to suffer an agonizing demise, but I can recognize that they wanted me to suffer the same fate, only to document the exact way I died. They want me to get myself killed so that they can understand.
==Entrances and Exits== Intentional entry to the level is unadvised unless performing a task that requires it. ===Entrances=== *Noclipping into certain surfaces within [[Level 877]] may transport one to this level. *Entering a monochromatic wooden door in [[Level 980]] will bring one here. *Noclipping into mirrors on any level has a very high chance to bring individuals here, though this method of entry is unadvised, as entering the mirrors will cause you to enter the level from a mirror, therefore initiating the Kaftrethanatos Process. ===Exits=== Egress from the level is achieved by using a door that opens to a random level with a number above 880; however, the door in question will not bear any indicator indicating it is the exit, meaning a thorough search is required to find the correct one. {{Collapse|num=2|initial=
'''> Author and Credits'''
|final='''There is nowhere for you to run.'''|body=











A deafening scream reverberates out from behind you as you use the small remaining energy left in you to escape its source. Each step you take creates loud, repetitive thumps on the carpet below, the recursion of such sounds driving you to insanity. The bloodthirsty creature behind you eagerly awaits your succumbing to the many factors hampering your ability to continue. Your breath is heavy, each inhale and exhale painfully forcing its way through your throat as sharp pains in your chest repeatedly pulse through your helpless body. But you can't stop running. All you have to do is put one leg in front of the other. Why is something so simple so hard to do? Demotivation sets in as you realize just how weak you are. You turn your head up slowly, your vision taking in a stoppage ending the hallway in front of you. A wooden door masked with dark gray paint is fixed to the wall in front of you, the only thing separating you from potential freedom from the beast behind you. The faster you sprint, the more pain you inflict on yourself. The end feels so close, yet so far. You raise your arm, tightly gripping the cold metal doorknob, and rotate your hand counterclockwise before thrusting the door open and taking a final step towards the room it once concealed. Before the entity can reach you, you slam your foot into its chest and slam the door behind you, turning the lock. The angry screams of the entity surge through the environment as you stumble towards a corner and sit down, your breath preventing you from comfort. You grab the zipper of your backpack and slowly pull it along its track, each collision with the bumps it passes forming a soft zipping noise as it reveals the backpack's contents. Reaching inside of it, you feel around in the uncovered compartment and grasp onto a handgun. Pulling it out reveals its muzzle facing you, reminding you of how hopeless you are. You could shoot the entity and continue your attempt to survive. But why? Regardless of your actions, your attempts will ultimately turn out futile. There's nothing left to work towards, no one left for you to exist for, and no ounce of joy can be obtained from what remains of your short life. Killing the entity will only prolong the inevitable, and it wants your body a lot more than you do. Shaking, you turn the gun to your head and place your finger on the trigger. You examine the world around you one last time and see that the entity has broken through the door. You can be liberated from all of the suffering you've felt, all the things you regret, and every single thing that hurts you just by the simple pull of a trigger. But what if there's an escape? Maybe you can avoid death entirely. Each step the entity takes towards you further increases your understanding that escape is not worth it. This is the easiest way you can escape. With the remaining seconds you have left, you take a deep breath, tighten your grasp on the handgun, and pull the trigger. But the gun doesn't fire. Realization that you forgot to turn off the gun's safety sinks in. But it is far too late to fix your mistake. The entity grabs your defenseless self and drives its arm through your chest, replacing every feeling in your body with excruciation. The pain only heightens as layers of your body get torn off slowly, but you're too exhausted to scream. The entity then ceases its attack and stands up, keeping eye contact with your body. So much of your body has been torn off, and the amount of pain you feel is incomprehensible. You just want the entity to end your suffering, but it won't give you that mercy. As the next moments seemingly stretch into an eternity, the suffocating sense of agony refuses to cease its assault. You can't relax, you can't breathe, and the only thing that exists is the relentless pain that engulfs you from every direction. And then, without warning, everything finally stops. The pain dissipates slowly, and nihility swallows your senses, leaving you engulfed in nothingness. . . . What are mirrors, exactly? At its core, the purpose of a mirror is easily described — it reflects. They display an identical copy of what lies before them. This manifestation of such a space, in a sense, can be described as duplication. This is consequently the reason why mirrors within many works of fiction have the ability to duplicate items; to create an exact copy of something. Additionally, in some cases, they may be portrayed as objects capable of creating an infinite number of these identical copies. This is what you realize when you open your eyes to see the exact same mirror that led to your previous demise.

Thank you for reading this page. I put a lot of effort into it, and I hope you enjoyed reading it more than I did making it :D }}}} [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Dangerous]] [[Category:Fear Anthology]] [[Category:Horror]]
